hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (residue 193/195) and TM5 (residue 208), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique TM6 (residue 272/273), thereby increasing the selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong ddG but poor structural quality and limited sequence diversity. Tightening structural constraints and explicitly targeting non-pharmacophore positions for selectivity optimization is critical to improve yield and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better structural packing and reduce candidates with poor geometry, likely increasing the structural pass rate.

Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5) will allow more precise mutation design to disrupt conserved pocket interactions while preserving the native pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
